meta.title: Chiang Rai Night Bazaar Guide | Night Market on Phahon Yothin Road
meta.description: Your complete guide to the Chiang Rai Night Bazaar: opening hours, how to get there, what to buy, bargaining tips, Northern Thai food, and a live map. Rated 4.2 from 15,199 Google reviews.

hero.subtitle: Chiang Rai's largest and most famous night market, along Phahon Yothin Road near the Golden Clock Tower and the Old City gate — handicrafts, souvenirs, Northern Thai food and nightly culture.
hero.plannerNote: Stalls open from about 6 PM. Arriving near dusk lets you watch the bazaar light up and beats the late-night crowds.
hero.weatherError: Live weather is unavailable right now

about.label: Get to know
about.heading: What is the Chiang Rai Night Bazaar?
about.lead: The Chiang Rai Night Bazaar is the city's best-known evening market, on Phahon Yothin Road close to the Golden Clock Tower and the Old City gate.
about.paragraph1: After dark the street fills with stalls selling handicrafts, textiles, jewellery, souvenirs and hill-tribe goods from the Akha, Karen, Lisu, Lahu and Hmong communities. There are also restaurants, Thai massage and street performances.
about.paragraph2: It is also a great starting point for nearby sights such as Wat Phra Kaew, the King Mengrai Monument and the Mae Kok River, making the market both a shopping spot and the heart of Chiang Rai's night culture.
about.quote: Come after dark — that's when the whole street lights up with stalls, food smoke and music.

highlights.title: Crafts & souvenirs
highlights.desc: Silk, silver, woodcarving and hill-tribe keepsakes

highlights.title: Northern food & street eats
highlights.desc: Khao soi, sai ua sausage, or vegetarian options

highlights.title: Thai massage & foot spa
highlights.desc: Ease tired legs with an affordable massage

editorTip.text: Arrive around 18:00–19:00 when it's cooler and the stalls are just lighting up, then settle in for dinner at the food court.

history.label: History & lore
history.heading: The story of Chiang Rai's night market
history.intro: The market grew alongside Chiang Rai's rise as a tourist base in the 1990s, when travellers began using the city as a stopover for the Golden Triangle — yet the area's historical and Lanna cultural roots run far deeper.

blocks.title: Lanna heritage
blocks.text: Chiang Rai was once the capital of the Lanna Kingdom and a long-standing caravan crossroads, so handicrafts, textiles, silver and hill-tribe goods have accumulated here for generations — the very soul of this night market.

blocks.title: The Golden Clock Tower
blocks.text: At the market's heart stands the Golden Clock Tower, designed by Thawan Duchanee, a National Artist from Chiang Rai. Its golden crown glows as the city's favourite meeting point and a symbol of modern Chiang Rai.

blocks.title: A night-time cultural hub
blocks.text: Today the market blends commerce, food and performance, expressing a modern Chiang Rai identity that still keeps its Lanna cultural roots.

blocks.title: Nearby historical landmarks
blocks.text: A short walk away is Wat Phra Kaew, the original home of the Emerald Buddha — Thailand's most revered image — alongside the King Mengrai Monument and the Mae Kok River, all anchoring the area's deep history.

legend.title: Local legend
legend.text: The striking Golden Clock Tower at the heart of the market was designed by Thawan Duchanee, a National Artist from Chiang Rai. Locals say its golden crown honours the glory of old Lanna, and it has become the meeting point everyone trusts.

didYouKnow.title: Did you know?
didYouKnow.text: On weekends the Walking Street expands the market area even further, making it especially lively.

sources.label: Sources & declaration
sources.text: This page is produced by volunteers as a non-profit educational guide. Historical and coordinate data are referenced from the sources listed in the 'Reference sources' section below and may be updated for accuracy.

guide.label: Visitor guide
guide.heading: How to visit the Chiang Rai Night Bazaar
guide.lead: Entry is free and wandering is easy. Here's what helps you get the most out of your visit.

scamTip.text: Cheap 'designer' goods are usually fakes — bargain to about half the asking price. For food, check the price on the sign before ordering.

items.title: Souvenirs
items.desc: From a few baht; bargaining expected

items.title: In town
items.desc: Walk from the Old City gate or Clock Tower in 10–15 minutes

items.title: Tuk-tuk / songthaew
items.desc: Red songthaews loop the city for 20–40 THB

items.title: Grab
items.desc: Easy to book via the app with clear pricing

guide.faqLead: Answers to the questions visitors ask most often.

faqs.q: What's the best time to visit?
faqs.a: Arrive around 18:00–19:00 when it's cooler and the stalls are lighting up; 20:00–22:00 is the liveliest.
Carry small cash and a power bank; some stalls are cash-only.
Walk from the Old City gate or Clock Tower in 10–15 minutes; red songthaews and Grab are handy too.
No — entry is free, perfect for a relaxed stroll.
Very — there's varied food and it's safe to stroll with kids.

food.label: Must-try eats
food.heading: Food at the Chiang Rai Night Bazaar
food.lead: The market mixes restaurants, a food court and roadside stalls, covering Northern Thai and street food in one place.

dishes.name: Khao soi
dishes.desc: Curry-noodle soup, a Northern Thailand classic

dishes.name: Sai ua
dishes.desc: Northern herb sausage with chili dip

dishes.name: Mango sticky rice
dishes.desc: A crowd-favourite sweet of ripe mango and warm sticky rice

quote.text: Shop till you're hungry, then grab a roadside plate — the smell of khao soi is the signal you've arrived in Chiang Rai.
quote.author: A local food blogger

gallery.label: In pictures
gallery.heading: The Chiang Rai Night Bazaar in photos
gallery.intro: A look at the bazaar after dark — the stalls, the food and the street lights.
gallery.historicalNote: All images show the real atmosphere of the Chiang Rai night market.
gallery.credits: Images are for illustration only and are not owned by this site.

reviews.label: From visitors
reviews.heading: Chiang Rai Night Bazaar reviews
reviews.intro: A summary of what real visitors say on Google Reviews.
reviews.declaration: Reviews shown are summarised for illustration, not verbatim posts.

featured.text: Such a fun market to walk around — souvenirs and food, perfect after dinner.
The khao soi here is delicious and cheap, and the vendors are friendly.

items.text: Lovely atmosphere, could walk for hours, so many souvenirs.
items.name: A. Suwan

items.text: Great variety of food; the sai ua and khao soi were my favourites.
items.name: K. Phrom

items.text: Bargain hard — some stalls quote high at first.
items.name: M. Lee

items.text: Quite crowded on Sat–Sun but still enjoyable.
items.name: T. Anan

items.text: Easy to reach, right by the Golden Clock Tower.
items.name: P. Mod

items.text: Arriving at 18:00 was just right, not too hot.
items.name: V. K

mapSection.label: Map & directions
mapSection.heading: How to get to the Chiang Rai Night Bazaar
mapSection.lead: The market sits in central Chiang Rai and is easy to reach by public transport or on foot.
mapSection.address: WR4M+5M2, Tambon Wiang, Mueang Chiang Rai District, Chiang Rai 57000
